text: For any project to succeed, you must set clear goals at the start. A useful approach is the SMART method, where goals are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. This makes it easier to track progress and avoid confusion. You should also break the project into milestones, which are key checkpoints that help you stay on schedule. Milestones are connected to deliverables, such as reports or prototypes, that you share with the team. Finally, stakeholder alignment is crucial. Everyone involved, from managers to team members, needs to agree on the goals and understand their roles. This ensures fewer surprises and smoother collaboration throughout the project.
